div.h1: Undo Order: Global LSN Priority Queue
div.p: Multiple active transactions must be undone in reverse LSN order globally, not per-transaction.

g.text: Undo T2 fully, then T3, then T5

ul.li: LSN 450 (T3) Undo 400 restores page 5 to "E". Undo 450 then restores to "K". But T3's before-image "K" assumes page 5 has T2's after-image "F"! Wrong value! Data corruption!

ol.li: LSN 400 (T2) - oldest modification Each undo sees the correct before-image because we undo in reverse chronological order across ALL transactions.

text.tspan: // Build priority queue from active transactions
// Push last_lsn with txn_id for tracking
// Always pop highest LSN across ALL txns
// Add prev record to queue if exists
// Build priority queue from active transactions
// Push last_lsn with txn_id for tracking
// Always pop highest LSN across ALL txns
// Add prev record to queue if exists

div.p: When multiple transactions modify the same page, each transaction's before-image assumes the page is in the state left by the previous modification. If T2 modified page 5 at LSN 400, and T3 modified page 5 at LSN 450, then:

ul.li: T3's before-image is the state after LSN 400
T2's before-image is the state before LSN 400 Undo T2 first? You overwrite T3's before-image. Undo T3 first? You restore correct intermediate state. Always undo most recent first (highest LSN).
